{"query":"TSMC capital expenditure capex 2026 forecast $60B $64B revised AI chip demand date","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1055,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://photoncap.net/p/the-more-silicon-wins-the-more-inp","title":"The More Silicon Wins, the More InP Sells: Why UMC and Tower Both Expanded 300mm on the Same Day","content":"1 day ago — On the call, management raised the full year dollar revenue growth outlook to slightly above 40% and lifted 2026 capex to $60B to $64B [3].
The same day, Tower committed roughly $3B of its own capital to a 300mm SiPh expansion in Japan, backed by government grants .
Two days later, TSMC reported Q2 results: $40.2B in revenue, 67.7% gross margin, HPC at 66% of sales .
And two weeks before all of that, the Korean government unveiled an 8.4GW AI datacenter plan worth 550 trillion won .
The question is what demand they both see ahead.
Those are TSMC’s Q2 numbers, out on July 16 .
UMC and Singapore fabless SILITH shipped their first mass-produced silicon photonics (SiPh) wafers from a 12-inch fab , and the same day Tower announced a government backed 300mm SiPh expansion in Japan.
Q3 revenue guidance is $44.6B to $45.8B , which at the midpoint is another 12% plus quarter over quarter.
This is the same company that said “above 30% growth, capex at the top of $52B to $56B” back in April .
On June 29, at a presidential office briefing, the Korean government announced a phase one plan to build 8.4GW of AI datacenters with SK (5GW), GS (2.4GW), and Naver (1GW) .
Announced AI datacenter projects worldwide total 190GW, with $5.5T of investment projected through 2030 .
That one was about coherent DCI between datacenters.
Why 12-inch matters: SiPh production has long straddled 200mm and 300mm.
TSMC has been growing COUPE, a captive SiPh effort attached to its own packaging.
Take the announced demand (TSMC’s 66% HPC mix, Korea’s 8.4GW, the global 190GW pipeline) and convert it into optical components."},{"url":"https://intellectia.ai/news/stock/asml-raises-2026-outlook-amid-strong-demand","title":"ASML Raises 2026 Outlook Amid Strong Demand","date":"2 days ago","content":"2 days ago — TSMC has raised its 2026 capital expenditure forecast to between $60B and $64B, up from $52B to $56B, indicating strong confidence in future ..."},{"url":"https://intellectia.ai/news/stock/xi-jinping-highlights-chinas-ai-cooperation-with-global-south","title":"Xi Jinping Highlights China's AI Cooperation with Global ...","date":"22 hours ago","content":"22 hours ago — TSMC has raised its 2026 capital expenditure forecast to between $60B and $64B, up from $52B to $56B, indicating strong confidence in future ..."}]}
